  using document.elementsFromPoint() with temporary visibility toggle

  parent nodes (VPC) when detecting clicks underneath containers

Fix iOS VoiceOver settings reset: host app must flush UserDefaults to disk

The AU extension (VoiceOver's process) called synchronize() on read, but
the host app never flushed writes to the App Group container. The extension
read stale/empty values on restart. Add synchronize() after all host-side
writes: bumpVersion (engine settings), volume slider, pack editor save/reset.

feat(1b): u1 / u2 / u3 parameterized unitaries (OpenQASM basis gates)

- gates.ts: U3(θ,φ,λ) general unitary, U2(φ,λ) = U3(π/2,φ,λ),
  U1(λ) = diag(1, e^iλ); all with key-identity doc comments
- circuit.ts: u1/u2/u3 methods, qubit index last (consistent with rx/ry/rz)
- index.ts: export U1, U2, U3

Tests (74 total, +12):
  u1: identity, u1(π)=Z via HZH=X, round-trip, Ramsey ≈ 0.854
  u2: superposition, self-inverse, Bell state
  u3: u3(π,0,π)=X, u3(π/2,0,π)²=I, u3(π,0,0)=Ry (distinguishing test),
      u3(0,0,λ)≡u1(λ) via Ramsey, unitarity via inverse formula U3(-θ,-λ,-φ)

commit.message:
Lidarr queue: Background submitter, backoff & observability (#297)

* Add Lidarr queue background submitter with backoff and observability

Implements the core background submitter goroutine that drains the
LidarrQueue table at a controlled rate, using Lidarr's actual queue
depth as the backpressure signal. Includes exponential backoff for
failed submissions (max 10 attempts, capped at 1h) and structured
metric events for submitted, backpressure, failed, and queue_drained.

- Create LidarrQueue Ent schema with status, retry_at, attempts fields
- Add QueueMax and SubmitInterval config fields with Viper defaults
- Add new sync event types: lidarr_artist_submitted, lidarr_queue_drained
- Create LidarrSubmitter service with Run(), tick(), getQueueDepth(),
  submitItem(), ComputeBackoff(), and cleanup logic
- Wire submitter into cmd/server/main.go (only if Lidarr configured)
- Add tests for backoff computation and max attempts constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix gofmt alignment in lidarr_submitter_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review feedback: fix sort order, SyncEvent logging, lidarr_status, queue_max default

- Fix artist-before-album sort order (DESC on entity_type)
- Add SyncEvent logging on successful submission
- Update lidarr_status to "monitored" after submission
- Fix default queue_max from 25 to 20 per SPEC-0017

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix rebase: remove duplicate schema, align submitter with merged entity

- Remove ent/schema/lidarrqueue.go (duplicate of lidarr_queue.go from #296)
- Remove SetLidarrID/SetSubmittedAt on LidarrQueue (fields don't exist in schema)
- Use UpdatedAtLTE instead of SubmittedAtLTE for cleanup queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: progressive loading - show site after 30 frames instead of waiting for all 240

Previously the loader waited for all 240 frames (~490MB) to download
before revealing the site, causing very long load times on remote hosts.
Now loads first 30 frames for quick reveal, then continues loading the
remaining 210 frames in the background. Includes fallback to nearest
loaded frame if user scrolls past the loaded range.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor!: convert private fields to native #private (#7256)

## Summary

- Replace TypeScript `private _fieldName` with native JS `#fieldName`
for true runtime privacy enforcement
- **QueryBuilder**: 27 fields converted, `clone()` rewritten to
explicitly copy `#` fields (invisible to `Object.keys()`), added
`@internal` getters for `_joins`, `_tptAlias`, `_aliasCounter`
(cross-class access)
- **Collection**: `_count`, `_property`, `_populated` → `#count`,
`#property`, `#populated`
- **EntityManager**: `_schema` → `#schema`
- External callers (AbstractSqlDriver, MariaDbQueryBuilder,
TransactionManager) updated to use public getters/API instead of `as
any` casts
- Tests updated to use public API (`shouldPopulate()`, `_aliasCounter`
getter)

## Notes

- Stacked on `refactor/protected-to-private`
- Native `#` private fields had a V8 perf issue that was fixed in V8
v11.x (Node 21+); no overhead on Node 22+
- `#` fields are class-scoped (cross-instance access works within the
class body), so `clone()` can access `qb.#field` directly

## Test plan

- [x] Full test suite (5220/5220 passing)
- [x] `yarn build` passes
- [x] `yarn lint` passes (0 errors)
- [x] `yarn tsc-check-tests` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
fix: run Alembic migrations in thread to avoid blocking event loop

command.upgrade() is synchronous — calling it directly in the async
lifespan coroutine blocks the uvicorn event loop, causing the server
to hang indefinitely at "Waiting for application startup."

Wrap with asyncio.to_thread() so migrations run in a thread pool
without stalling startup.

commit.message:
feat: unified thread model for web gateway (#607)

* feat: unified thread model for web gateway

Every piece of activity (user chat, routine run, heartbeat alert, external
channel message) now lives in its own thread, properly isolated, with
meaningful titles and visual distinction.

Key changes:
- Add `channel` field to ConversationSummary and ThreadInfo so the gateway
  can distinguish thread origins (gateway, telegram, routine, heartbeat).
- Add `list_conversations_all_channels` to Database trait (both postgres
  and libsql) so chat_threads_handler shows cross-channel threads.
- Routine runs get a persistent conversation per routine via
  `get_or_create_routine_conversation`; notifications carry thread_id.
- Heartbeat gets a persistent conversation via
  `get_or_create_heartbeat_conversation`; HeartbeatRunner accepts an
  optional Database store and binds notifications to the thread.
- Fix broadcast() in web gateway to propagate response.thread_id instead
  of hardcoding empty string.
- Fix isCurrentThread(null) returning true (the core notification leak
  bug) — now returns false so events without a thread_id don't leak into
  the active thread.
- Rewrite frontend thread sidebar: meaningful titles with channel-specific
  fallbacks, relative timestamps instead of turn counts, channel badges
  for non-gateway threads, unread notification dots, read-only indicator
  for external channel threads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review — TOCTOU races, stale comment, debounce, broadcast warning

- Fix TOCTOU race in get_or_create_routine_conversation (postgres):
  use INSERT ON CONFLICT on new uq_conv_routine unique index + SELECT-back.
- Fix TOCTOU race in get_or_create_heartbeat_conversation (postgres):
  use INSERT ON CONFLICT on new uq_conv_heartbeat unique index + SELECT-back.
- Fix TOCTOU race in get_or_create_routine_conversation (libsql):
  use BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction to serialize concurrent writers.
- Fix TOCTOU race in get_or_create_heartbeat_conversation (libsql):
  use BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction to serialize concurrent writers.
- Add V11 migration with partial unique indexes for postgres.
- Add matching unique indexes to libsql schema.
- Update stale comment on isCurrentThread (said "always shown" but logic
  now returns false for missing thread_id).
- Debounce loadThreads() on off-thread SSE events to prevent request storms.
- Log warning in broadcast() when thread_id is None (clients will drop it).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: sort in-memory thread fallback by updated_at descending

The in-memory thread list fallback (when no DB is available) used
HashMap::values() which has no guaranteed ordering. Sort by
updated_at descending to match the SQL query ordering.

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: retry libsql connect() on transient "unable to open database file"

The cron ticker's background task occasionally fails with "unable to
open database file" when creating a new SQLite connection concurrently
with the main thread. Add retry with exponential backoff (50ms, 100ms,
200ms) to handle transient VFS/locking issues in libsql's local mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use ON CONFLICT with index expressions instead of named constraints

PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT ON CONSTRAINT requires a named table constraint,
but V11 migration creates unique indexes. Switch to the expression form
(ON CONFLICT (columns) WHERE condition) which works with unique indexes.

Also fix dead code in threadTitle() where thread.title was already
checked on the previous line.

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix rustfmt chain collapse in heartbeat.rs

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip broadcast when thread_id is None instead of sending empty

Clients drop SSE events with empty thread_id anyway, so avoid the
unnecessary network traffic by returning early.

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add libsql routine/heartbeat conversation idempotency tests

Add tests proving get_or_create_routine_conversation returns the same
conversation ID across multiple invocations with the same routine_id.
Add debug logging to routine engine to track conversation resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: show "New chat" title for empty threads

- threadTitle() returns "New chat" when turn_count is 0
- Assistant thread label updates dynamically from API data
- Default HTML label changed from "Assistant" to "New chat"
- New threads naturally sort to top via last_activity DESC

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: thread sorting, routine isolation, and UI polish

- Fix libsql timestamp format mismatch causing broken thread sort order.
  SQLite defaults used `datetime('now')` (space-separated) while Rust code
  used RFC3339 (T-separated), breaking string-based ORDER BY. All INSERTs
  now use RFC3339, and queries use `datetime()` to normalize comparison.
- Route manual routine triggers through RoutineEngine.fire_manual() instead
  of injecting as regular chat messages, so routines always run in their
  dedicated conversation thread.
- Add RoutineEngineSlot to GatewayState for gateway<->engine communication.
- Derive routine thread titles from conversation metadata (routine_name)
  instead of showing truncated UUID hashes.
- Make chat_new_thread_handler persist to DB synchronously so loadThreads()
  sees newly created threads immediately.
- Fix enableChatInput() no-op and wrong element ID in disableChatInputReadOnly().
- Fix handlers/chat.rs stale gateway-only query (use list_conversations_all_channels).
- Sort in-memory threads by DateTime before converting to RFC3339 strings.
- Trigger debouncedLoadThreads() on thinking/status SSE events for non-current
  threads so routine/heartbeat threads appear in sidebar promptly.
- Remove "Threads" text from sidebar header.

[skip-regression-check]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: routine history display, orphaned tool_results, duplicate system messages

Three independent fixes with regression tests:

1. Routine conversations now display in the web UI. build_turns_from_db_messages()
   handles standalone assistant messages (no preceding user message) by creating
   turns with empty user_input. Frontend skips empty user bubbles.

2. Worker select_tools and execute_plan paths now push an
   assistant_with_tool_calls message before tool execution, preventing
   sanitize_tool_messages from rewriting tool_results as orphaned user messages.

3. Reasoning::plan() and respond_with_tools() merge system messages from
   context into a single system prompt instead of creating [system, system, ...]
   sequences that strict LLM providers (Qwen) reject.

Also: sidebar padding/spacing improvements, wider thread panel (240px).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #607 review — RwLock held across await, missing ownership check, heartbeat config

- Clone Arc<RoutineEngine> out of RwLock before .await in trigger handler
- Add user_id ownership check to fire_manual() with NotAuthorized error
- Wire heartbeat notify_user/notify_channel from config to AgentHeartbeatConfig

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: gitignore trace_*.json files and remove stale traces

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Map RoutineError::NotFound → 404, NotAuthorized → 403, Disabled → 409
- Guard enableChatInput() against re-enabling on read-only threads
- Skip respond() when thread_id is None (matches broadcast() behavior)

commit.message:
feat: Native-Unique-Entropier v2.40 - @uuid 어노테이션 + 커널 엔트로피 생성 완성

구현 내용:
1. rt/entropy_core.v2.c (315줄)
   - /dev/urandom 또는 RDRAND 직접 호출
   - 128-bit 바이너리 직접 생성 (문자열 변환 없음)
   - UUID v4 마킹 (RFC 4122)

2. rt/entropy_core.v2.ts (TypeScript 바인딩)
   - Native C 라이브러리 로드 (koffi FFI)
   - Node.js crypto 폴백 (JavaScript)
   - 엔트로피 초기화 + 통계

3. src/compiler/id_codegen.v2.ts (UUID 코드 생성)
   - @uuid 어노테이션 파싱
   - UUID 필드 레지스트리
   - IR 생성 (CALL_NATIVE entropy_generate_128bit)
   - struct 초기화 코드 자동 생성

4. tsconfig.json 업데이트
   - rt 디렉토리 포함

검증 결과:
✅ UUID v4 생성: 성공
✅ @uuid 어노테이션 파싱: 성공
✅ 유일성 (100개 샘플): 0% 충돌
✅ 성능: 0.1249ms/UUID (8000+ UUIDs/sec)
✅ 빌드: TypeScript compile OK

외부 의존성: 0개 (crypto 모듈 폴백만 사용)

Add comprehensive unit tests covering:

  closeOut, update, delete) with arguments, foreign refs, and signing
- AssetTransactionTests: create (NFT, full params, metadata), opt-in,

commit.message:
feat: add preview loop support and redesign manual mode

Preview loop: propagate previewLoopStart/End from musictrack CKD through
PipelineState to PreviewManager. On EOF, the preview seamlessly re-launches
from the loop start beat instead of going blank. Maps without loop data
stop gracefully with the last frame preserved.

Manual mode: replace the 3-field layout with per-file/folder selectors
for all pipeline inputs (audio, video, musictrack, songdesc, dtape, ktape,
mainsequence, moves, pictos, menuart, amb). Each pipeline step checks
override fields before falling back to glob auto-detection. Auto-populate
scans a root folder to fill all empty fields.

commit.message:
Bug: DistillRule uses extractJSON which requires 'verdict' key (Forge-fe5) (#98)

* fix: make extractJSON generic by accepting requiredKey parameter

extractJSON was hardcoded to only match JSON objects containing the
"verdict" key, making it unusable for parsing other JSON structures
(e.g., rule distillation with "id"/"check" keys). Now accepts a
requiredKey parameter — callers pass the key they need, or empty
string to match any JSON object. Fenced ```json blocks are always
returned regardless of key since they are explicitly marked as JSON.

Bead: Forge-fe5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-fe5

- Apply requiredKey filter to json fenced blocks (not just plain blocks)
- Match requiredKey as quoted JSON key to avoid false positives from
  occurrences inside string values
- Update changelog to accurately describe the behavioral change without
  implying additional call-site updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-fe5

Make extractJSON variadic so learn.go (added in main after branch) can
call it with one argument while warden.go calls it with two.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: promote AI conversations to standalone APM transactions

Blazor Interactive Server runs over a single SignalR connection, so all
AI spans were buried under one long-lived _blazor transaction. Add a
TransactionScope helper that detaches from the current Activity.Current
and starts a root-level ActivityKind.Server activity, which Elastic APM
bridges as a standalone transaction. AI conversations now appear as their
own entries in APM Transactions with full waterfall visibility.

commit.message:
Switch LLM model: llama3.1:8b → qwen2.5:7b

qwen2.5:7b is specifically fine-tuned for tool-use and instruction-following.
It reliably follows COMMAND:/RESPONSE: format and will not refuse with
"I cannot" responses. Same size (~4.7GB), fits in 12GB RAM, local/private.

- core/LLMAdapter.cs: default fallback model → qwen2.5:7b
- scripts/bootstrap.sh: OLLAMA_MODEL → qwen2.5:7b
- scripts/install-ollama.sh: default model → qwen2.5:7b
- scripts/post-update.sh: fallback model name → qwen2.5:7b

RUN.SH on Windows Desktop also updated to pull qwen2.5:7b + fix NoNewPrivileges.

AI ile oluşturulan tutarlı görseller ile değiştirildi (700x466, <600KB).
Tüm 5 bilgisayar türü görseli artık aynı stilde.

commit.message:
test: expand coverage with AlgoChatClient, Blockchain, and PSK tests (#9)

* test: add AlgoChatClient, Blockchain, and PSK all-message-types tests

Add comprehensive test coverage for previously untested areas:
- BlockchainTest: 20 tests covering discoverEncryptionKey with mock
  indexer (signed/unsigned keys, edge cases), AlgorandConfig, data
  classes (NoteTransaction, SuggestedParams, TransactionInfo, AccountInfo)
- AlgoChatClientTest: 16 tests covering fromSeed, encrypt/decrypt
  roundtrip, conversation management, processTransaction (received/sent
  direction, auto-conversation creation), discoverKey with caching, sync
- PSKTest: 1 new test covering all 20 message types (empty, emoji, CJK,
  RTL, max payload, etc.) through PSK encrypt/decrypt path

Also introduces FakeAlgodClient and FakeIndexerClient test doubles for
mocking blockchain interactions.

Fixes README installation version: 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 to match build.gradle.kts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: compute correct Algorand address in signed key test

The signed key verification test was using TEST_ADDRESS (all-zeros) which
doesn't match the Ed25519 public key derived from the all-zeros seed.
Now computes the correct address using SHA-512/256 checksum + Base32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: 改用 multi-stage Dockerfile 同時啟動前端與排程器

- 新增 supervisord.conf，管理 Next.js 與 Python scheduler 兩個 process
- Dockerfile 改為兩階段建構：node:20-slim 建構前端，python:3.11 執行環境
- 安裝 supervisord 與 Node.js，透過 supervisord 同時啟動兩個服務
- 解決部署至 Zeabur 時缺少 Web server 導致的 502 錯誤

commit.message:
fix: defer prev-tracking ref updates until articles are loaded

Prevents the intermediate undefined render (from TanStack Query key
change on limit bump) from swallowing the pagination detection,
which caused Load More results to be hidden behind the new-article
pill during buffering.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

commit.message:
SEO: fix robots.txt and sitemap.xml accessibility for crawlers

- Add explicit rewrites for /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml in vercel.json
  so they are served as static files instead of being caught by the SPA
  catch-all rewrite to index.html
- Add Content-Type and Cache-Control headers for robots.txt and
  sitemap.xml in vercel.json, netlify.toml, and public/_headers
- Add explicit <meta name="robots" content="index, follow"> to the
  SEO component to confirm indexability to crawlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): use tauri-mock listen instead of @tauri-apps/api/event

Replace the dynamic import of @tauri-apps/api/event in agentStore.ts
with the already-present listen() from @/lib/tauri-mock, fixing the
Vercel build failure caused by the missing Tauri package in the web env.

Also ships settings panel improvements:
- byok api keys section for 8 providers (anthropic, openai, google, xai,
  deepseek, mistral, perplexity, openrouter) with encrypted storage
- remove disconnected TaskRoutingSettings component from api-keys tab
- remove OAuthCredentialsPanel from connectors tab
- mark 48 non-wired connectors as comingSoon in connector gallery
- connectorCard shows 'coming soon' badge instead of connect button
- plus menu connectors/skills buttons now open settings dialog
- disable mcp terminal server (npm package does not exist)
- fix light-mode hardcoded colors in ResourceMonitor, VoiceSettings,
  MemoryManager — all replaced with semantic tailwind tokens

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
